title: eriogonum wrightii var. trachygonum
intro: Eriogonum wrightii var. trachygonum, commonly known as redstem buckwheat, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of redstem buckwheat include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
